title: Undo vs confirmation dialog
summary: Use undo when recovery is reliable and the action is common. Use confirmation when the action is high-consequence and cannot be safely reversed.
decisionRules: Prefer undo for frequent, low-risk actions that users may perform quickly.
Prefer confirmation for irreversible, expensive, security-sensitive, or uncommon destructive actions.
Do not use confirmation dialogs as a substitute for clear labels, previews, or recovery.
failureModes: Using confirmation for every routine action creates habituation.
Using undo for irreversible external side effects creates false confidence.

sources.note: Apple's alert guidance distinguishes uncommon irreversible actions from undoable destructive actions.
